violations.text: A knife was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Knife was brought to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a garbage can. The soap and paper towel at the hand-washing sink at the bar was blocked by equipment. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Items were relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knife) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Knife was brought to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed portioned rice at 56 degrees in the walk-in. Rice was moved to freezer to rapidly chill. Containers of recently cooked chicken were observed stacked; containers were placed in a single layer. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
ROP salmon was observed thawing with package intact. Salmon was 37 degrees; staff removed from packaging. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (in direct contact with bottles at the bar, top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. All scoops were removed to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
Storage containers were not allowed to air dry prior to stacking. PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The wall in the near the bar hand-washing sink has an accumulation of soil residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2025
The following is in poor repair: 1. Temperature gauges for dishwashing machine (COB: 06/14/2024) 2. Glass washer at main bar (COB: 04/14/2024) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/14/2024

The hand-washing sink at the secondary bar was lacking paper towels. Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink at the secondary bar. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

Raw animal foods (such as whole eggs and liquid eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cheese and pickles, and cake) in the walk-in refrigerator and reach in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer and blenders at bar) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Equipment was cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher at main bar was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC informed all glass ware to be cleaned and sanitized in 3 bay sink or kitchen dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

Various foods were observed held at between 44-51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods that were out of temperature for an unknown amount of time were voluntarily disposed of. See attached disposal. Foods in prep unit were observed stored in double pans. PIC informed to cease practice to allow proper air flow in unit to food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water at the bar between uses. Dipper well was turned on during time of inspection Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024
Temperature gauges for dishwashing machine were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
